title: Attribute sensitivity sweep
summary: Sweep one or more attribute values across products and return a results row for every (product, attribute, value) point.
description: Runs the choice simulator once per distinct (product, attribute, test-value) triple defined in scenario_chgs, stacking all results into a single output CSV. This produces a classic sensitivity chart dataset: how does each product's share change as one attribute is varied at a time, holding everything else at the scenario baseline?

arguments.name: measures
arguments.description: Pipe-separated output measures. Any subset of: share, revenue, volume, cost, profit, standard_error. Use "__all__" for all six.

arguments.name: scenarios
arguments.description: Names of scenarios to run. Use ["__all__"] for every scenario in the sim_db.

arguments.name: subgroups
arguments.description: Subgroup column names. Use ["__all__"] for every subgroup column. When omitted, only total is returned.

arguments.name: weights
arguments.description: Weight column names. Use ["__all__"] for all weights.

arguments.name: throttles
arguments.description: Throttle-set name(s). Omit to use the sim_db default.

arguments.name: considerations
arguments.description: Consideration-set name(s). Omit to use the sim_db default.

arguments.name: include_nets
arguments.description: When true, append net rows summed by product_net group.

arguments.name: exponents
arguments.description: Beta scale factor applied before exponentiation.

arguments.name: prob_methods
arguments.description: Probability computation method(s). Single method or a pipe-separated blend (e.g. "sop|top2"). Methods: "sop" — standard share-of-preference (multinomial logit); "topN" where N ≥ 1 — top-N restricted MNL, only the N highest-utility products receive non-zero probability ("top1" is equivalent to first choice); "<top_spec>:<bot_spec>-<att>" — two-level nested logit where products are grouped by scenario attribute <att>, with <bot_spec> within groups and <top_spec> across groups (e.g. "sop:sop-Brand", "top1:sop-Category"); "<bot_spec>-<att>" shorthand sets top_spec=sop implicitly. Blend methods with | and weights via prob_methods_wgts.

arguments.name: prob_methods_wgts
arguments.description: Blend weights corresponding to each method in prob_methods when blending multiple methods. Weights are normalised to sum to 1.

arguments.name: trips
arguments.description: Monte Carlo shopping trips per respondent. Must be >= 1.

arguments.name: price_formulas
arguments.description: Eval-lang expressions for derived price attributes, applied before each sweep point.

arguments.name: scenario_chgs
arguments.description: Attribute sweep values keyed by product_name. Each attribute value should be an array of numbers — one simulation point per element. A scalar is treated as a one-point sweep. Example: {ProductA: {price: [8.0, 9.0, 10.0]}}.

arguments.name: threads
arguments.description: Rayon worker threads for the points × scenarios × weights fan-out. `1` (default) runs serially. Each sweep point is scored independently, so parallel speedup scales with the sweep size. Results are deterministic regardless of thread count.

details:
For each product P, each attribute A with an array of values [v1, v2, v3, ...], the command generates one override point: {P: {A: vi}}. All other products and attributes are held at the scenario baseline. The scenario column in the output is tagged as "<scenario_name>|<product>|<attribute>|<value>", making it easy to pivot on product or attribute in downstream analysis.

If scenario_chgs contains a single number (not an array) for an attribute, that attribute is treated as a one-point sweep, producing a single override row for that product/attribute combination.

Multiple attributes on the same product are swept independently (not jointly): for ProductA with price = [8, 9, 10] and quality = [1, 2], the command generates 5 points — 3 for price and 2 for quality — not 6 for their cartesian product. Use sim_combinations for joint sweeps.

When no scenario_chgs are supplied, the command degrades gracefully to a plain sim_run.
value: Writes one CSV file (sim_sensitivities_0.csv) with all sweep points stacked. Scenario labels encode the sweep point: e.g., "base|ProductA|price|9.00". In protocol mode emits event.run.started, event.artifact.created, event.run.finished.
notes: Each sweep point overrides only the named product's named attribute; all other products and attributes remain at baseline.
To sweep the same attribute across multiple products simultaneously (coupled price movement), use sim_lock_step instead.
Multiple attributes on the same product are swept independently, not as a joint grid. Use sim_combinations for the joint case.

see_also.description: Cartesian product or zip sweep across multiple products simultaneously.
Move multiple products in lock-step through parallel value lists.

fields.name: measures
fields.description: Pipe-separated measures. Default: share|revenue.

fields.name: scenarios
fields.description: Scenario name(s) to run. Use ["__all__"] for every scenario.

fields.name: subgroups
fields.description: Subgroup column name(s). Use ["__all__"] for all.

fields.name: weights
fields.description: Weight column name(s). Use ["__all__"] for all.

fields.name: throttles
fields.description: Throttle set name(s). Omit to use sim_db default.

fields.name: considerations
fields.description: Consideration set name(s). Omit to use sim_db default.

fields.name: include_nets
fields.description: Add net rows summed by product_net group. Default: false.

fields.name: exponents
fields.description: Beta scale factor applied before exponentiation. Default: 1.0.

fields.name: prob_methods
fields.description: Probability method: sop, topN (N≥1), nested <top>:<bot>-<att>, or pipe-blended. Default: sop.

fields.name: prob_methods_wgts
fields.description: Blend weights for prob_methods. Default: [1.0].

fields.name: trips
fields.description: Monte Carlo shopping trips per respondent. Default: 1.

fields.name: trip_seeds
fields.description: Random seed for trip simulation. Default: 100.

fields.name: price_formulas
fields.description: Eval-lang expressions for derived prices.

fields.name: return_debug
fields.description: Include per-respondent debug output. Default: false.

fields.name: scenario_chgs
fields.description: Sweep values: {product: {attr: [v1, v2, ...]}}.

fields.name: threads
fields.description: Rayon worker threads for points × scenarios × weights fan-out. Default: 1.

artifacts.title: Sensitivity sweep results
artifacts.description: Simulation results from sweeping one attribute at a time across products, holding everything else at baseline. Shows how each product's share or revenue responds as attributes (such as price) are varied — the classic sensitivity chart dataset.

fields.name: scenario
fields.description: Sweep point label, e.g. 'base|ProductA|price|9.00'. Identifies which attribute was changed and to what value.

fields.name: subgroup
fields.description: Respondent subgroup.

fields.name: product
fields.description: Product being measured.

fields.name: measure
fields.description: Metric type (share, revenue, etc.).

fields.name: value
fields.description: Simulated value at this attribute level.

fields.name: error
fields.description: Standard deviation across posterior draws.
